questions.text: The story 'Three Questions' was written by:
questions.explanation: Leo Tolstoy is a famous Russian author known for this philosophical short story.

questions.text: What was the most important time according to the hermit?
questions.explanation: The most important time is 'Now' because it is the only time we have power to act.

questions.text: Who is the most important person?
options: The person you are with at a particular moment
questions.explanation: The most important person is the one with whom you are at any given moment.

questions.text: What is the most important business/work?
options: To do good for the person you are with
questions.explanation: The purpose of life is to do good for others.

questions.text: The King went to visit the hermit in:
questions.explanation: The hermit only saw common folk, so the King put on simple clothes.

questions.text: When the King reached the hermit's hut, the hermit was:
questions.explanation: The hermit was digging the flower beds in front of his hut.

questions.text: Who was the bearded man who came running out of the woods?
questions.explanation: The man was the King's enemy who had planned to kill him.

questions.text: Why did the bearded man ask for the King's forgiveness?
questions.explanation: The King dressed his wounds and saved him even though he was an enemy.

questions.text: The hermit gave his answers to the King's questions:
options: After the King helped the wounded man
questions.explanation: The hermit waited for the King to experience the answers through his actions.

questions.text: Which question was the King NOT seeking an answer to?
options: What is the right time to begin something?
Which people should he listen to?
How can he become the richest King?
What is the most important thing to do?
questions.explanation: The King wanted to know how to be successful and never fail, not specifically about wealth.
